entry.title: FBREF Data Scraping Walkthough pt.3
entry.summary: In part two of the data scraping walk through, we successfully achieved the following items; Create a set of working functions to aggregate data from FBREF. Perform a series of data munging tasks to get easy to to use datasets ready for analysis. Create a series of Data Visualisations from these cleaned datasets. Assess the meaningful metrics we need to st...

entry.title: FBREF Data Scraping Walkthough pt.2
entry.summary: In part one of the data scraping walk through, we successfully achieved the following items; Create a set of working functions to aggregate data from FBREF. Perform a series of data munging tasks to get easy to to use datasets ready for analysis. Create a series of Data Visualisations from these cleaned datasets. Although there is still one action not currently...

entry.title: FBREF Data Scraping Walkthough pt.1
entry.summary: Code and notebook for this post can be found here. In my previous post, which you can find here, I outlined the current data landscape in the football analytics world including my picks for the best free resources out there. This post is a part of series of posts, where we will explore how to use web-scraping packages available in python to get football data as efficiently as possible. This ...

entry.title: Show me the Data. pt.2
entry.summary: The Data Landscape In short, the data landscape for sports analytics is flush with options at our disposal. There is a healthy mix of open sources as well as the big players in the data game asking for a pretty penny to get you in the know. Then there are the medium sized to smaller operations that operate semi-free or for small(er) fees. And then there are the grassroots operations scraping e...

entry.title: Show me the Data. pt.1
entry.summary: “Without data you’re just another person with an opinion.” This simple quote is one of, if not my favourite quote that encapsulates what I’m trying to do with this blog and even at greater extent the kind of assertion I hope to make in this journey. So before I even think about loading VS Code. I need to acquire the right, high quality data in order to answer my problem statement. ‘Can we ide...
